learning_outcomes.description: demonstrate critical skills in dealing with primary ancient sources and solving the problems presented by such material
show considerable ability to carry out independent research, managing the information obtained, and communicating your findings in a coherent and scholarly way
recognise, interpret, and contextualise distinctive features of the material culture of the ancient Greeks and Romans
participate in activities which involve group discussion as well as individual effort

assessments.task_description:
There are ten tutorials in ANCH2301, starting in Week 2. A detailed Tutorial Programme, including reading lists, will be made available via Blackboard. You should sign up for a tutorial group before Week 1 and remain in that group for the entire semester. Tutorials are conducted in person and are not recorded.

Tutorials are an integral part of the course, since they offer the opportunity for small-group discussion of specific topics, close analysis of ancient evidence, and completion of individual and group activities. Exam questions will also address material covered in tutorials.

You are expected to prepare for each tutorial by completing the required reading and preparing notes based on the discussion questions for each week. The questions are designed to guide your reading and preparation for the tutorial. It is recommended that you bring notes on paper (handwritten or printed) if you wish to refer to them during class, as some activities will be completed without use of electronic devices (laptops, tablets, phones, etc.). Please note that use of AI tools during tutorials is prohibited and relying on AI tools is likely to hinder your learning and skill-development.

Tutorial activities will vary by week and may include worksheets, creative exercises, reflections, source analysis, and mini quizzes. Some activities are designed to be completed in small groups and some individually. Some will be completed 'closed book' and/or without use of electronic devices (secure assessment). Details of each activity will be provided in class. These activities will not be graded separately but will be taken into account in evaluating the quality and extent of your preparation for and engagement in tutorials.

Tutorial participation and activities are worth 20% of your overall mark for the course. You are expected to participate in (and therefore attend) all ten tutorials. While missing one or two tutorials over the semester will not have a serious impact on your mark, poor attendance will limit your learning as well as your participation mark, and failure to participate in at least one tutorial will result in an automatic grade of 2. That is, you must participate in at least one of the ten tutorials in order to pass the course.

Because tutorial exercises are secure assessment, they must be completed in person. If you are unable to attend due to illness or misadventure and you wish to make up participation for that week, please contact the course coordinator as soon as possible to discuss alternative arrangements and obtain a medical certificate (if relevant). Alternative arrangements may include attending a make-up session or an oral interview. It is not acceptable to send notes instead of participating in person unless by prior arrangement with the course coordinator.

If you have an SAP that may impact how you engage in this assessment, please contact the course coordinator as soon as possible to discuss appropriate accommodations.


AI: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date: The essay must be submitted through Turnitin by 2pm Friday in the week after the tutorial week at which that topic was discussed The exact due date for each essay topic can be found in the Tutorial Programme. Please take account of the due date when choosing an essay topic and plan your time accordingly.
assessments.task_description:
Task: Students are required to research and write a scholarly essay on one of the Essay questions included in the Tutorial Programme (on Blackboard). The essay questions are linked to the weekly tutorial topics. You will be asked to sign up for an essay topic at the start of semester. You only need to write one essay.

Please note: If you choose a Greek topic for your tutorial essay (tutorials 2-6) you must choose a Roman topic (tutorials 7–10) for your final exam essay, or vice versa (if you choose a Roman tutorial essay topic, your final exam essay must be on a Greek topic).

Referencing Style: Students must use the Classics and Ancient History Referencing style. Guidance on structuring your essay and referencing will be provided on Blackboard..

Word Limit: 2000 words (including discursive footnotes) excluding references and bibliography.

A word count that is within ±10% of the set length (word limit) is acceptable. Word count does not include footnotes; however, discursive footnotes are included in the word count. A word count that is outside these 10% will receive a proportionate penalty and will be graded against the grading criteria.

Submission: The essay must be submitted through Turnitin by 2pm Friday in the week after the tutorial week at which that topic was discussed The exact due date for each essay topic can be found in the Tutorial Programme and on Blackboard. Please take account of the due date when choosing an essay topic and plan your time accordingly.

A reading list for each topic can also be found in the Tutorial Programme and through Talis on the library website. It is essential that you use the Required Reading in writing your essay, including the ancient sources.

AI: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Please refer to UQ rules for using AI in assessment

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit your completed Essay through the appropriate submission link on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The final exam will be held on campus during the exam period. The exam will consist of short-answer and multiple-choice questions, a source analysis exercise, and an essay. Exam questions will be based on lecture and tutorial content.

Please note: If you choose a Greek topic for your tutorial essay, you must choose a Roman topic for your essay in the final exam, and vice versa: if you choose a Roman tutorial essay topic, your exam essay must be on a Greek topic. Failure to do so will result in a mark of 0 for the exam essay.

Further details will be provided in class and on Blackboard.

You must sit the exam in order to pass the course.

AI: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
